name: OpenGov Government Cloud API
what_it_does: OpenGov Government Cloud REST API for cloud-based public sector budgeting, procurement, permitting, and financial management. Enables AI agents to manage operating budget development and capital improvement planning for government finance automation, handle procurement and bid management for public sector purchasing compliance, access permit and license management for community development workflow automation, retrieve financial reporting and CAFR preparation for government financial compliance, manage performance management and KPI tracking for government accountability, handle citizen engagement and public transparency for government communication, access fund accounting and GAAP government compliance for public sector accounting, retrieve payroll and HR integration for public sector workforce management, manage inspection and code enforcement for regulatory compliance automation, and integrate OpenGov with state and local government ERP systems and legacy platforms.
use_cases: Automating government budgeting from finance agents
Managing permit applications from community development agents
Processing procurement bids from purchasing agents
Generating financial reports from compliance agents
not_for: Federal government agencies beyond state and local scope
Private sector organizations without government accounting needs
International governments outside US state and local focus
best_when: A US state or local government using OpenGov wants AI agents to automate budget development, procurement, permitting, financial reporting, and performance management.
avoid_when: PUBLIC RECORDS AND TRANSPARENCY RISK: Government data processed through OpenGov is subject to state public records laws (FOIA, state sunshine laws); automated data management must account for records retention schedules and public records request obligations. Government procurement compliance — automated procurement processes must comply with public bidding laws, including advertised bid requirements, bid opening procedures, and award criteria; automated procurement shortcuts that bypass required bidding processes create procurement law violations. GASB accounting standards — automated government financial reporting must comply with GASB (Government Accounting Standards Board) fund accounting principles; automated journal entries without GASB compliance review create CAFR inaccuracies and audit findings.

auth.notes: OpenGov uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Redwood City, California HQ. Founded 2012. Private (Cox Enterprises, Andreessen Horowitz, Emerson Collective, Thrive Capital). 1,500+ government customers across US state and local government. Cloud ERP for public sector. Competes with Tyler Technologies, Accela, and Munis for government ERP and permitting.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — government subscription pricing
pricing.notes: Redwood City, California. Founded 2012. Cox/a16z/Emerson-backed. 1,500+ government customers. Cloud government ERP. Competes with Tyler Technologies and Accela.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; government SLA documented

known_agent_gotchas: PUBLIC RECORDS LAW: Government data in OpenGov is subject to state FOIA and sunshine laws; automated data management must account for records retention schedules, e-discovery obligations, and public records request response requirements

Public procurement law compliance — automated purchasing must comply with state and local competitive bidding thresholds, advertised bid requirements, and award procedures; automated procurement that bypasses required public bidding creates procurement law violations

GASB fund accounting — government financial reporting requires GASB fund accounting principles; automated journal entries must maintain fund integrity and comply with GASB standards for governmental accounting
StateRAMP/FedRAMP requirement — government agencies require StateRAMP or FedRAMP authorization for cloud services; verify current OpenGov authorization status before agency procurement

Permit public notice requirements — automated permit processing for certain development projects requires public notice, comment periods, and hearing procedures under state planning and zoning laws; automated permit approval without required public process creates legal challenge risk

Government fiscal year and budget cycle — government budget workflows are tied to annual fiscal year cycle; automated budget processes must respect budget adoption and amendment procedures under state budget law
agent_readiness.security_notes: Government cloud. SOC2, FedRAMP, StateRAMP. OAuth2 with scopes. US. Government financial and civic data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Government cloud. SOC2, FedRAMP, StateRAMP. OAuth2 with scopes. US. Government financial and civic data.
